title: Young Socceroo reportedly headed to German club
summary: Promising midfielder Paul Okon-Engstler is reportedly headed to Germany, the first of an emerging batch of Socceroos to move to bigger leagues.
imageAlt: Young Socceroo reportedly headed to German club
aiSummary: Promising midfielder Paul Okon-Engstler is reportedly headed to Germany, the first of an emerging batch of Socceroos to move to bigger leagues.

fullArticle:
Australia's Paul Okon-Engstler had an impressive World Cup with the Socceroos. 

  (Reuters: Albert Gea)

Young Socceroo Paul Okon-Engstler is reportedly headed to German club FC Köln.

A transfer fee of 1 million euros ($1.64 million) has been reported to sign the 21-year-old away from Sydney FC.

European football journalists Fabrizio Romano and Florian Plettenberg say a medical and signing are to be conducted next week.

Promising midfielder Paul Okon-Engstler is reportedly headed to Germany, the first of an emerging batch of Socceroos to move to bigger leagues.

The 21-year-old, who impressed in Australia's World Cup campaign, will join Bundesliga club FC Köln from Sydney FC.
